title: Bricklayers
description: Lay and bind building materials, such as brick, structural tile, concrete block, cinder block, glass block, and terra-cotta block, with mortar and other substances, to construct or repair walls, partitions, arches, sewers, and other structures. Build stone structures, such as piers, walls, and abutments. Lay walks, curbstones, or special types of masonry for vats, tanks, and floors. Assemble, install, or maintain solar photovoltaic (PV) systems on roofs or other structures in compliance with site assessment and schematics. May include measuring, cutting, assembling, and bolting structural framing and solar modules. May perform minor electrical work such as current checks. (Based on 10 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Replace broken or missing masonry units in walls or floors. Clean excess mortar or grout from surface of marble, stone, or monument, using sponge, brush, water, or acid. Mix mortar or grout and apply it to marble slabs, stone, or foundation. Remove wedges, fill joints between stones, and finish joints using a trowel for an attractive finish. Inspect brickwork or structure to determine need for repair and interpret blueprints to determine specifications and calculate materials required. Lay brick to build chimneys and other structures, ensuring vertical and horizontal alignment using tools such as plumb bob and levels. Repair cracked or chipped areas of stone or marble, and remove rough spots from concrete, using a blowtorch and power grinder.
Set stone or marble in place according to layout or pattern and shape, trim, and cut stone or marble preparatory to setting, using power tools and hand tools.

Construct and install prefabricated masonry units, dig trenches for foundations using hand tools, and assemble, install, or maintain photovoltaic (PV) systems in accordance with codes, ensuring correct orientation and structural integrity. Perform routine maintenance on PV systems and test voltages to ensure functionality.

Lay out wall patterns or foundations, and calculate angles and courses to determine alignment for laying bricks, blocks, or tiles. Remove excess mortar with trowels, hand tools, and finish mortar joints for a uniform appearance.
Perform tasks related to masonry and its installation, such as applying and smoothing mortar or mixtures, preparing site conditions, and cleaning working surfaces.

Drill holes in ornamental stone and anchor brackets in holes, shape and install anchor materials to bind building materials securely, and create strong construction for various applications in masonry.

Layout, assemble, and install various types of fences, gates, and barriers, ensuring structural integrity and alignment. This includes digging postholes, setting posts in upright positions, and securing rails.
Identify and remove hazardous materials during construction activities and decontaminate work areas, ensuring compliance with safety regulations.
Check and maintain cleanliness of tools and work areas.
Provide consultations with customers regarding masonry work, renovations, and project scopes.
Mix specified amounts of sand, clay, mortar powder, and water to form refractory clay or mortar, using shovels or mixing machines.
